existing_Entity_Description: Cranbrook is a municipality in Kootenays, British Columbia, Canada, within the East Kootenay. As of the 2021 Canadian Census, Cranbrook had a population of 20,499. The community is situated in a mountain paradise of tight-knit communities nestled between dramatic peaks and. a niche market with loyal local clientele and growing tourism. The region features snowy winters ideal for skiing and warm summers for lake activities.

REGION_BUNDLE.climate: Interior mountain climate with heavy snowfall (the Powder Highway ski belt), wet-cold winters and warm summers; steep freeze-thaw cycling and snow-shed engineering; lake-moderated pockets around Nelson and Creston are gentler.
REGION_BUNDLE.terrain: Parallel north-south ranges (Selkirks, Purcells, Monashees, Rockies) separated by long lakes (Kootenay, Arrow, Slocan) and river valleys; communities cling to lakeshores and valley floors.
REGION_BUNDLE.economy: Teck's smelter at Trail (one of the world's largest zinc-lead smelters), mining heritage towns, forestry, hydro dams on the Columbia, ski tourism (Revelstoke, Fernie, Rossland), and Nelson's arts economy.
REGION_BUNDLE.building_stock: Some of BC's best-preserved heritage streetscapes (Nelson, Rossland, Fernie), miners' cottages, lakefront cabins, and mountain homes built for metres of snow.
